stimulus: In 2007, more than 150 years after the death of Ada Lovelace, the Association for Computing ______ the contributions of the trailblazing 19th-century mathematician and author of the first published computer algorithm, establishing an annual award for early-career women in computing in her honor.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It is the only choice that supplies a form of the verb "recognize" that can join with the subject "the Association for Computing Machinery" to form a complete sentence. When narrating from a point of view in the past, "would" can be used to express something that happened later: more than 150 years after Lovelace's death, the ACM would finally recognize her contributions.

Choice A is incorrect because it produces a sentence fragment; the participle "recognizing" cannot serve as the main verb of the clause. Choice B is incorrect because the to-infinitive "to recognize" cannot serve as the main verb, leaving the sentence without one. Choice D is incorrect because the participial phrase "having recognized" cannot serve as the main verb, producing a sentence fragment.
